stepInstruction:
Verify Windows nodes are ready
oc get nodes -l kubernetes.io/os=windows

Query controllerConfig for certificate data
oc get controllerconfig machine-config-controller -o yaml

Extract and verify kubelet CA certificate
oc get controllerconfig machine-config-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.kubeAPIServerServingCAData}' | base64 -d

Verify kubelet-ca.crt exists on Windows nodes
Get-Item C:\k\kubelet-ca.crt

Verify cloud provider CA (if configured)
Check: oc get controllerconfig machine-config-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.cloudProviderCAData}'
If configured: Get-Item C:\k\ca-bundle.crt
stepExpectedResult:
If configured: file exists and matches controllerConfig data
If not configured: validation skipped (e.g., AWS)

stepInstruction:
Verify user CA bundle (if configured)
Get-ChildItem -Path Cert:\LocalMachine\Root | Measure-Object
stepExpectedResult:
If configured: certificates in Windows store, count > 0
If not configured: validation skipped

stepInstruction:
Verify kubelet service is running
Get-Service kubelet

Verify Windows nodes remain Ready
oc get nodes -l kubernetes.io/os=windows -o wide
stepExpectedResult: All Windows nodes Ready, no certificate errors
